items.title: June 2026 newsletter
items.summary:
The June edition of my sponsors-only monthly newsletter is out. If you are a sponsor (or if you start a sponsorship now) you can access it here.
This month:

Claude Fable 5, GPT-5.6, and US export restrictions
GLM-5.2 is the new best open weights model
Tokenmaxxing is so over
Datasette Apps
sqlite-utils and shot-scraper and Datasette
Miscellaneous WASM projects
Other model releases
What I'm using

Here's a copy of the May newsletter as a preview of what you'll get. Pay $10/month to stay a month

items.title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
AI’s Volatile Power Use Quietly Tests Grid Limits
items.summary: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure is typically framed as an energy problem. Data centers are projected to consume a growing share of global electricity demand: The International Energy Agency estimates they could account for 3 to 4 percent of total global consumption within this decade.Utilities are already adjusting long-term forecasts to accommodate anticipated growth from hyperscale facilities and high-density compute clusters.This framing captures scale. It misses

items.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: The great loops debate and the state of AI engineering
items.summary: The AI Engineer World’s Fair ended with a debate about loops, a report on the state of AI engineering, and closing keynotes focused on what to build next.

items.title: Vercel's Andrew Qu on why agents are a new kind of software
items.summary: The Vercel Chief of Software explains how its agent framework, eve, was created — and why skills, sandboxes and agent-readable websites now matter.

items.title: The website of the future may assemble itself for every visitor
items.summary: Adobe is experimenting with “agentic sites” that generate pages around an individual user’s intent. At AIEWF, we talked to Carlos Sanchez about the Web's future.

items.title: llm-coding-agent 0.1a0
items.summary:
Release: llm-coding-agent 0.1a0
        Another Fable 5 experiment. Now that my LLM library has evolved into more of an agent framework it's time to see what a simple coding agent would look like built on it.
I started a new Python library using my python-lib-template-repository GitHub template repository, then ran these two prompts (here's the Claude Code for web transcript):

Write a spec.md for this project - it will depend on the latest “llm” alpha from PyPI and implement a Claude code style

items.title: Using DSPy to evaluate and improve Datasette Agent's SQL system prompts
items.summary:
Research: Using DSPy to evaluate and improve Datasette Agent's SQL system prompts
        One of this morning's AIE keynotes covered dspy, which reminded me I've been meaning to see if it could help me improve the system prompt used by Datasette Agent - so I fired off an asynchronous research task in Claude Code for web using Claude Fable 5:

Pip install the latest Datasette alpha and datasette-agent and dspy - then figure out how to use dspy to evaluate and improve the main system prompts

items.title: Understand to participate
items.summary:
I saw Geoffrey Litt speak at AIE yesterday, and one framing he used particularly resonated with me:
Understand to participate
Geoffrey was talking about the challenge of collaborating with coding agents as they construct increasingly large and sophisticated changes, and the need to avoid taking on cognitive debt as your understanding drifts from how the code actually works.
His argument is that you need to understand the code to a depth that enables you to participate further with the model:

Yo

items.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
items.summary: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how work should flow across departments. Both offered a repeatable way to…

items.summary: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.

items.title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
items.summary: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.

items.title: Skill engineering and the case against one-shot AI design
items.summary: Paul Bakaus talks to us about Impeccable, human judgment in a 'loopmaxxing' era, and why agents still need people to steer them.

items.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
items.summary: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety are paramount, AI is becoming a core operating layer. With its sprawling industrial systems and constant stream of operational…

items.title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
items.summary: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.

items.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Autoresearch and the tension between AI and human agency
items.summary: The software factory vision met resistance today from speakers defending human understanding and control.
